title: Erdős #291 companion, part (ii) (Steinerberger): gcd(aₙ,Lₙ)>1 infinitely often
informal_statement: Erdos #291 (open): with L_n = lcm(1,...,n) and a_n defined by sum_{1<=k<=n} 1/k = a_n / L_n, is gcd(a_n, L_n) = 1 for infinitely many n? This packet proves only part (ii), the EASY already-known direction attributed to Steinerberger: gcd(a_n, L_n) > 1 occurs for infinitely many n. This does NOT resolve the open part (i) (whether gcd(a_n,L_n)=1 infinitely often), which remains genuinely open.
informal_proof_idea: Take n = 2*3^k (k >= 1). Then the 3-adic valuation of L_n is k, so in a_n = sum_{j=1}^n L_n/j every term L_n/j is divisible by 3 except the two with 3^k | j, namely j=3^k and j=2*3^k. Writing L_n = 3^k*M (3 does not divide M, 2 divides M), those two contributions sum to M + M/2 = 3*(M/2), divisible by 3. Hence 3 | a_n; also 3 | L_n, so 3 | gcd(a_n,L_n) > 1. The map k -> 2*3^k is injective, giving infinitely many such n.
formal_statement_pp: theorem erdos_291_infinite_gcd_gt_one : {n : ℕ | 1 < Nat.gcd (a n) (L n)}.Infinite

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop; statement cross-checked against the sibling repo's already-kernel_verified proof of the same result

notes: ANTI-OVERCLAIM: this packet proves only the EASY, already-known companion direction (part ii) of Erdos #291, not part (i) (whether gcd(a_n,L_n)=1 infinitely often), which remains genuinely OPEN. Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 2f8943e6, problem_version 6a5fd25f-bf69-4354-957e-e2546df3be86), submitted as a SubmitModule (2 defs L/a + 5 helper theorems + root theorem), kernel_verified on the fourth submission -- the most complex module transported in this corpus's frontier lane so far (8 items). Failures 1-3 were all instances of the documented flattened-sequence trap (issue #67) at a larger scale than previously seen: helper module items are ALWAYS transported through the flattener regardless of requested proof_format, and any `have h := by tac1; tac2` assignment followed by more sibling tactics gets its trailing tactics silently swallowed into the inner by-block unless explicitly parenthesized as `have h := (by tac1; tac2)`. Attempt 2 fixed this for the `filter_multiples` helper (which also needed its two internal case-split branches rewritten bullet-free, using `Or.elim`/`.elim` term-mode dispatch instead of `·`-bullets, since bullet scoping does not survive the same forced flattening); attempt 4 fixed the same unparenthesized-by pattern recurring four more times in the `three_dvd_a` helper (prime3, hj_ne, e1, e2). The root theorem itself used `proof_format: raw_lean_block` successfully with its original bullet structure intact (root theorems are NOT forced through the flattener). Independently re-verified in THIS environment's own pinned toolchain rather than treated as evidence merely because the sibling repo (F:\Github\mnehmos.llm-driven-proof-search.environment\ErdosProblems\erdos-291\proof\Erdos291_infinite_gcd_gt_one.lean) already reports it kernel_verified there -- per this repo's evidence policy. training.eligibility: quarantined per the default-quarantine policy for open_problem_related packets pending real external review (same policy as the #399/#349/#494 packets).
